name: Fermi galactic-center gamma-ray excess
summary: Fermi-LAT data show an extended gamma-ray emission from the inner Milky Way at 1-3 GeV (peak ~ 2 GeV), spherically symmetric around Sgr A* with a flux ~ 10^-9 erg/s/cm^2, that is not explained by known diffuse sources, pulsars, or supernova remnants in standard astrophysical models. Two leading interpretations: (1) dark-matter annihilation chi chi -> bbar (m_chi ~ 30-50 GeV, sigma v ~ 10^-26 cm^3/s - thermal WIMP cross-section); (2) unresolved population of millisecond pulsars in the Galactic bulge. Bartels et al. 2016 wavelet analysis and Lee et al. 2016 non-Poisson template fitting favor PSR origin; Linden et al. 2016 and later reanalyses dispute statistical claims.

characteristic_numbers.expected: no excess in standard diffuse model
characteristic_numbers.note: Spectrum bumps at 1-3 GeV; consistent with annihilation through b-bbar final state for m_DM ~ 35-50 GeV.
Within a factor of 2 of the canonical thermal WIMP cross-section - the 'too good to be true' coincidence that gives the WIMP interpretation persistent traction.

principles_stressed.note: WIMP interpretation in mass range that is now strongly constrained by direct-detection (LZ 2024) and collider monojet searches; survives only in restricted Higgs/Z-mediated regions. Pulsar interpretation requires a Galactic-bulge MSP population an order of magnitude larger than predicted by globular-cluster extrapolation. Either way, our models are incomplete.

history: First identified by Goodenough and Hooper (2009), 'Possible evidence for dark matter annihilation in the inner Milky Way from the Fermi Gamma Ray Space Telescope', arXiv:0910.2998. Confirmed by Hooper-Goodenough (2011, arXiv:1010.2752), Daylan et al. (2014, arXiv:1402.6703). MSP interpretation: Abazajian (2011), Bartels-Krishnamurthy-Weniger (2016, PRL 116, 051102, arXiv:1506.05104), Lee-Lisanti-Safdi-Slatyer-Xue (2016, PRL 116, 051103, arXiv:1506.05124). Statistical-methodology debates 2017-2024 unresolved.

references.citation: Goodenough and Hooper, 'Possible evidence for dark matter annihilation in the inner Milky Way from the Fermi Gamma Ray Space Telescope', arXiv:0910.2998 (2009)
Bartels, Krishnamurthy, Weniger, 'Strong support for the millisecond pulsar origin of the Galactic Center GeV excess', Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 051102 (2016)
